sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) hinder part, rear, end. Arab. أُخُرُ id. Hence-(a) מֵאָחֹור from behind, behind, opp. to מִפָּנִים 2Sa 10:9. Arab. مِنْ أُخُر.-(b) לְאָחֹור backward, Psa 114:3, Psa 114:5with averted face (abgewandt), Jer 7:24.-(c) בְּאָחֹור backward; Pro 29:11, “a fool uttereth all his anger, וְחָכָם בְּאָחֹור יְשַׁבְּחֶנָּה but a wise man keepeth it back,” drives it back, so that it returns to himself.-(d) אָחֹוד in acc. adv. Arab. أُخُراً behind, on the back, opp. to פָּנִים and קֶדֶם. Eze 2:10, “and it (the roll) was written פָּנִים וְאָחֹור before and behind,” within and without; 1Ch 19:10 Psa 139:5. Also, backward; Gen 49:17, וַיִּפּל רֹכְבֹו אָחֹור “and his rider falleth backward;” Jer 15:6. So often pleon. after verbs of returning, Psa 9:4 56:10 of turning back, 2Sa 1:22 Psa 35:4 40:15 and others of the same kind. Pl. hinder parts, Exo 33:23 26:12 1Ki 7:25 Eze 8:16.

(2) the west [the east being the quarter towards which one is supposed to look], Job 23:8 Isa 9:11, וּפְלִשְׁתִּים מֵאָחֹור “and the Philistines on the west.” Compare קֶדֶם, יָמִין, תֵימן, שְׂמאֹל and C. B. Michaëlis, Diss. de locorum differentia ratione anticœ, posticœ, dextrœ, sinistrœ, Hale, 1735, 4 to, reprinted in Pott Sylloge Comment. 5, 80, seq. § 8. The same mode is followed by the Hindoos, the Mongols, and also the Irish [and all Celtic nations].

(3) latter time, the future. לְאָחֹור hereafter, Isa 41:23 42:23 .
